student_name: Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari
hub_subtitle: Inglês do dia a dia — apresentar-se, rotina e conversação

lesson.menu_desc: Sharing your opinion in everyday conversation -- how to say what you think, agree, disagree politely, and soften your words -- 33 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 15 -- Making Your Voice Heard
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ana Paula Porto de Oliveira Fornazari | Lesson 15 -- Making Your Voice Heard
lesson.grammar_point: expressing opinions, agreeing, disagreeing and hedging

listenings.text: People often ask me what I think about social media, and honestly, my point of view is complicated. In my opinion, it has some real advantages. I would argue that it helps us stay in touch with family far away, and I have to admit that I learn a lot from it every day. On the other hand, I want to point out one problem. Many people spend hours online without noticing, and I suppose that is not very healthy. Some of my friends insist that social media makes us lonelier, not closer. I am not sure I completely agree, but they have a point. If you ask me, the answer is balance. I actually think the app is just a tool; the real question is how we choose to use it. That is my honest opinion, but I understand that other people see it differently.

Hi Ana Paula, it is Sarah! I finally finished that series everyone keeps bringing up, and I have to give you my opinion. Honestly, I did not love it as much as the reviews said. In my opinion, the first three episodes are great, but after that the story gets slow. I know a lot of people disagree with me, and I admit the acting is wonderful. Still, if you ask me, a good story matters more than beautiful images. My brother argues the opposite, of course; he insists it is the best show of the year. I suppose we will never agree on this one. Anyway, I would love to hear your point of view. Do you think I am being too harsh, or do you see it the same way? Call me back and tell me honestly what you think. Bye!

extra_audio.text: First, bring up the topic and say what you are talking about. Then give your opinion clearly, with 'In my opinion' or 'I think'. After that, add a reason or point out a fact that supports your idea. Next, listen to the other person and agree or disagree politely. Finally, soften your words or find some common ground to keep the conversation friendly.
